name: MitoImmune urolithin A immune-aging trial
summary: A randomized double-blind placebo-controlled trial testing 1000 mg daily urolithin A for 4 weeks on immune-cell subsets, immune metabolism, and immune-cell function in healthy middle-aged adults.
population: 50 healthy adults aged 45 to 70 years randomized to urolithin A or placebo for 28 days

trial_details.horizon_note: Results are already represented by PMID 41174221; ClinicalTrials.gov has no posted results section.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for Microbiome Metabolite Restoration, Selective Autophagy and Organelle Quality Control, and Mitophagy Enhancers because they can show whether the intervention changes immune-cell biomarkers and mechanistic endpoints in healthy middle-aged adults. That is still narrower than proof of broad aging reversal.
